query: Lee Jae-myung approval rating Gallup Korea Realmeter late June early July 2026, is approval still falling, what is driving the decline, any dead cross
answer:
I've completed the research. Here's the bottom line:

**Is approval still falling? Yes.** As of the latest polls available July 1, 2026:
- **Realmeter (June 29):** 46.5% approval / 49.5% disapproval — the **6th straight weekly decline**, down from a ~65.5% peak in mid-April.
- **Gallup Korea (June 26):** 51% approval / 41% disapproval — the **lowest since Lee took office**, down 6pp in two weeks.

**Any dead cross? Yes — in Realmeter.** The first dead cross since inauguration appeared on **June 22, 2026** (approval 46.7% vs. disapproval 49.7%, within margin of error) and persisted on June 29. **Gallup has not yet crossed** (still a 10pp approval lead) but the gap is narrowing fast.

**What's driving the decline:**
1. National Election Commission (선관위) ballot-mishandling scandal from the June 3 local elections (most-cited factor)
2. Economy/livelihood — weak won/high exchange rate, cost of living
3. Housing policy
4. Ruling-party internal conflict and prosecutorial-reform disputes
5. Semiconductor/Samsung investment controversies
6. Collapsing conservative support (Gallup conservative approval fell to 32%)

Notably, there's a "decoupling" — the Democratic Party's support held up even as Lee's personal approval fell, suggesting the drop is read as a verdict on Lee/government management rather than a broad partisan shift.

sources.title: Lee's approval rating falls for sixth straight week: poll
sources.snippet: Lee's approval rating fell for a sixth consecutive week to 46.5 percent, remaining below the 50 percent mark for the second straight week, according to a Realmeter poll released Monday.

sources.title: Lee's approval rating sinks to lowest point since taking office: Gallup poll
sources.snippet: Lee's approval rating dropped to 51 percent, marking its lowest level since he took office in June last year.

South Korean president's approval falls for sixth week

key_facts: Realmeter June 29, 2026 poll (survey June 22-26, n=2,502): Lee approval 46.5%, disapproval 49.5% — 6th consecutive weekly decline, 2nd straight week below 50%.
Realmeter June 22, 2026 poll (survey June 15-19, n=2,517): approval 46.7% vs disapproval 49.7% — the FIRST dead cross since Lee took office, within margin of error.
Gallup Korea June 26, 2026 poll (survey June 24-26, n=1,000): approval 51% (-6pp), disapproval 41% (+6pp) — lowest approval since Lee took office in June 2025; no dead cross yet.
Realmeter approval fell from a peak of 65.5% (mid-April) to 46.5% over roughly six weeks.
Gallup approval fell from 64% (May) to 57% (June 12) to 51% (June 26).
In Realmeter, opposition People Power Party (42.0%) now narrowly leads the ruling Democratic Party (41.0%) as of late June.
Main driver cited: National Election Commission (NEC) ballot-mishandling scandal tied to the June 3, 2026 local elections.
Other drivers: economy/high won exchange rate/cost of living, housing policy, ruling-party internal conflict, prosecutorial-authority disputes, semiconductor/Samsung investment controversies.
Conservative-leaning approval in Gallup collapsed to 32% (from 41%), with disapproval rising to 65%.
A 'decoupling' noted: Democratic Party support held/rebounded slightly even as Lee's personal approval fell.
